protocols.description: A set of strings representing HTTP protocols.

timeout.description: Connection timeout with the guardrail service.

block.description: template or string to evaluate block field

block_message.description: template or string to evaluate block_message field

response.description: Configuration specific to parse guardrail response.

block_reason.description: Metric to indicate the reason for blocking the input.

block_detail.description: Metric to indicate the detail for blocking the input.

masked.description: Metric to indicate whether the input was masked.

params.description: Parameters to be used in the guardrail service request. Keys are the parameter name and values can be either Lua expressions in the form `$(some_lua_expression)`or string. For expression, it will be evaluated as the value for the corresponding key. For string, it will be attempted to be parsed as string in JSON format, otherwise it will be used as is.

url.description: the url string or a template to generate one

body.description: A map used to evaluate a JSON object. Keys are the field names in the new object, and values can be either Lua expressions in the form `$(some_lua_expression)`or string. For expression, it will be evaluated as the value for the corresponding key. For string, it will be decoded as string in JSON format or be used as is.

headers.description: A map used to evaluate a JSON object. Keys are the field names in the new object, and values can be either Lua expressions in the form `$(some_lua_expression)`or string. For expression, it will be evaluated as the value for the corresponding key. For string, it will be decoded as string in JSON format or be used as is.

queries.description: A map used to evaluate a JSON object. Keys are the field names in the new object, and values can be either Lua expressions in the form `$(some_lua_expression)`or string. For expression, it will be evaluated as the value for the corresponding key. For string, it will be decoded as string in JSON format or be used as is.

value.description: Specify the full token value for 'name'.

location.description: Specify whether the 'param_name' and 'param_value' options go in a query string, or the POST form/JSON body.

request.description: Configuration specific to guardrail request. Fields below support template evaluation. Warning: if template is used, please verify that the client is from a trusted source to prevent injection.

functions.description: Custom functions to be used in expression templates.

custom_metrics.description: A list of custom metrics to be recorded.

proxy_scheme.description: The proxy scheme to use when connecting. Only `http` is supported.

auth_username.description: The username to authenticate with, if the forward proxy is protected by basic authentication.

auth_password.description: The password to authenticate with, if the forward proxy is protected by basic authentication.

no_proxy.description: Comma-separated list of hosts that should not be proxied.

http_proxy_host.description: A string representing a host name, such as example.com.

http_proxy_port.description: An integer representing a port number between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

https_proxy_host.description: A string representing a host name, such as example.com.

https_proxy_port.description: An integer representing a port number between 0 and 65535, inclusive.

ssl_verify.description: Whether to verify the SSL certificate of the guardrail service endpoint.

guarding_mode.description: The guardrail mode to use for the request.

allow_masking.description: Allow masking the request/response instead of blocking it. Streaming will be disabled if this is enabled.

response_buffer_size.description: The amount of bytes receiving from upstream to be buffered before sending to the guardrail service. This only applies to the response content guard.

text_source.description: Select where to pick the 'text' for the guardrail service request.

stop_on_error.description: Stop processing if an error occurs.

consumer.description: If set, the plugin will activate only for requests where the specified has been authenticated. (Note that some plugins can not be restricted to consumers this way.). Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the authenticated Consumer.

consumer_group.description: If set, the plugin will activate only for requests where the specified consumer group has been authenticated. (Note that some plugins can not be restricted to consumers groups this way.). Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the authenticated Consumer Groups

route.description: If set, the plugin will only activate when receiving requests via the specified route. Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the route being used.

service.description: If set, the plugin will only activate when receiving requests via one of the routes belonging to the specified Service. Leave unset for the plugin to activate regardless of the Service being matched.
